game_name: Joe & Mac 2: Lost in the Tropics

design_philosophy.core_loop: Defeat Neanderthals and dinosaur bosses across themed levels, collect rainbow stones, return to hub map to select next level and manage village relationships
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Combat encounters against enemy patterns and climactic boss battles with dinosaur creatures
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Stone Age caveman ninja warrior mastering prehistoric combat and bonding with village companions through courtship and family building
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid action-platformer with narrative relationship mechanics (courtship, marriage, fatherhood) integrated into progression via stone wheel currency and level replay. Combines side-scrolling action combat with overhead RPG-style level selection and village management.

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives per player (Joe or Mac selected at start)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Players can replay levels to accumulate stone wheels (currency) for purchasing gifts and advancing relationships, suggesting optional grinding for progression momentum

progression.upgrade_system: Stone wheels serve as currency for purchasing gifts (flowers, meat) to advance relationship status with girlfriend characters in village
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat boss enemies to earn rainbow stones; final level requires defeating all previously-encountered bosses sequentially before final boss Gork encounter
replayability_hooks: Replay levels to accumulate stone wheels for relationship progression
Multiple girlfriend options in village to pursue (player agency in romantic choice)
Sequence break potential in final level with boss gauntlet mechanic

world_building_techniques: Prehistoric Stone Age village hub setting with NPC interaction
Dinosaur and Neanderthal enemy encounters establishing world threats
Themed level environments (valley, jungle, snowy mountain, swamp, volcano) suggesting exploration narrative
Boss creatures as environmental obstacle/narrative encounters

boss_design.philosophy: Dinosaur creature encounters serving as level climaxes and progression gates; pattern-based combat encounters with repeated boss rematches in final gauntlet
notable_bosses: Gork (final boss with two-phase transformation: defeated once, gains power from crown and transforms into warthog demon, defeated again to claim crown and win)

faction_indicators: Village community (girlfriend selection represents social bonding faction)
Joe vs Mac player identity (two selectable protagonists)
danger_signals: Dinosaur boss encounters indicate escalating threat levels
Final gauntlet of all previous bosses signals climactic danger
Gork transformation mechanic signals hidden/adaptive threat
environmental_storytelling: Six distinct biome levels (valley, jungle, snowy mountain, swamp, volcano) suggest territorial expansion narrative
Friendly creatures (Pteranodon, Styracosaurus, Plesiosaurus) offering ride mechanics indicate environmental alliance possibilities
Stone wheel currency ties player economy to level completion and relationship investment
Village hub with marriageable NPCs establishes settlement narrative
